title: 10 best herbal teas for over bleeding during periods
intro_desc: Herbal teas for over bleeding during periods are a natural remedy that helps to regulate excessive menstrual flow, reducing discomfort and anxiety. These teas contain herbs such as chamomile, calendula, and cramp bark, which work together to soothe the uterine lining, reduce inflammation, and ease cramps. Examples of herbal teas that help with over bleeding include Raspberry leaf tea, Peppermint tea, and Ginger tea. Drinking these teas can improve lives by reducing the need for pads or tampons, decreasing fatigue, and promoting overall well-being during menstruation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose teas helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural antispasmodic and astringent properties. The tea's active compounds, such as rutin and quercetin, help to constrict blood vessels and reduce capillary fragility, thereby decreasing menstrual flow. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its ability to alleviate heavy bleeding by reducing inflammation in the uterine lining. As a result, guelder rose teas can provide relief from excessive bleeding and associated discomfort during menstruation.
remedy_properties: Astringent compounds: The tea's astringent compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, help to reduce blood loss by contracting the uterine muscles and constricting the blood vessels, thereby slowing down bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Guelder Rose tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm the uterus, reducing inflammation and spasms that may contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.

Antioxidant activity: The tea's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and flavonoids, help to protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive bleeding, promoting overall uterine health and reducing menstrual cramps and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Guelder Rose contain tannins and flavonoids, which help to contract blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding during menstruation.
Barks: The barks of Guelder Rose contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help to prevent oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, reducing the severity of menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh viburnum opulus leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Rinse the harvested plant material with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Combine one teaspoon of dried viburnum opulus leaves and flowers with 200ml boiling water for five minutes steeping.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup discarding solids carefully.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before drinking in small amounts as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover teas helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains isoflavones, which have been shown to mimic the body's natural estrogen levels. This helps to regulate blood flow and reduce heavy bleeding associated with menstruation. Additionally, red clover has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the uterine lining, further reducing excessive bleeding. As a result, red clover teas may provide relief from distressing symptoms of menorrhagia, making it a natural and effective remedy for women experiencing heavy periods.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover tea contains isoflavones, which have been shown to help reduce blood flow and ease menopausal symptoms, including heavy bleeding during periods. This is because isoflavones work as natural estrogen receptors in the body, regulating hormonal imbalances that can contribute to heavy bleeding.

Vitamin K: Red clover tea is a rich source of vitamin K, which plays a crucial role in blood clotting and platelet function. Adequate vitamin K levels can help reduce menstrual bleeding by promoting healthy coagulation and preventing excessive bleeding.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in red clover tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy bleeding during periods.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and isoflavones, which help to tone and constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive menstrual flow.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, they can help to reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, alleviating heavy bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried red clover flowers of high quality according to your trusted supplier or gather them yourself sustainably.
Measure one teaspoon of dried red clover flowers and add it to a clean glass teapot.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 212°f and pour it evenly over the red clover flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the bioactive compounds and essential oils.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with over bleeding during periods because its soothing properties can help to ease menstrual cramps, reduce inflammation, and relax the uterine muscles. This can lead to a decrease in heavy bleeding and clotting. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may also help to reduce swelling and irritation within the uterus, allowing for a more normal and manageable flow.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Chamomile tea helps to constrict blood vessels, which can reduce excessive bleeding during menstruation by reducing blood flow to the uterus.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to soothe and calm the uterine lining, reducing inflammation that may contribute to heavy or prolonged bleeding.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic effects can help to relax uterine muscles, which can reduce cramping and spasms that may exacerbate bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce heavy menstrual flow and alleviate cramps.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have natural anticoagulant properties, which can help to regulate blood clotting and reduce excessive bleeding during periods.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried chamomile flowers and measure out one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Heat fresh filtered water in a tea kettle or pot until it reaches boiling point then reduce heat to simmer.
Steep the chamomile flowers in the hot water for 5-7 minutes allowing their soothing properties to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes then drink it slowly throughout the day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai teas helps with over bleeding during periods because it has a profound effect on the uterine blood vessels, helping to regulate blood flow and reduce excessive menstrual discharge. The tea's natural constituents, such as ferulic acid and isoflavones, have anti-inflammatory properties that ease cramping and constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing heavy bleeding. Additionally, dong quai has been shown to balance the body's hormones, which can also contribute to a more normal and manageable menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: Dong Quai's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ferulic acid and kaempferol, help reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which can alleviate heavy bleeding.

Blood vessel constriction: The bioactive compounds in Dong Quai tea, including flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow to the uterus and thereby decreasing menstrual bleeding.

Hormone regulation: Dong Quai's saponin content has been found to regulate hormonal balance, particularly estrogen levels, which can help normalize menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding by promoting a more balanced uterine environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of dong quai have been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders and heavy bleeding due to its ability to regulate blood flow and alleviate symptoms.
Rhizomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the plant, rich in antioxidants and other beneficial compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote blood clotting to stop excessive bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried angelica sinensis roots in amounts of one to two teaspoons per cup of boiling water.
Heat pure filtered water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil for five minutes.
Measure and add the dried angelica sinensis roots to the boiling water, allowing them to steep for eight to twelve minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids after three to five minutes.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it immediately, with recommended dosages ranging from one to two cups per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with over bleeding during periods because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce blood platelet aggregation and improve blood flow. The curcumin present in turmeric tea also has a natural ability to stabilize the uterus, reducing uterine contractions that can lead to heavy bleeding. Additionally, turmeric's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties may help prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate excessive bleeding. By consuming turmeric tea during periods, women may experience a reduction in bleeding volume and duration, promoting a more comfortable and manageable menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heavy menstrual bleeding, allowing for easier healing and reduced symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric tea's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by excessive bleeding, reducing the risk of anemia and promoting overall health during this time.

Blood clotting regulation: The active compounds in turmeric tea, particularly curcuminoids, have been shown to regulate blood clotting and platelet activity, helping to reduce excessive bleeding and promote normal menstrual flow.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome, being the underground stem of the turmeric plant, contains a high concentration of bioactive compounds like curcuminoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote blood clotting, making it effective in managing heavy menstrual bleeding.
Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce excessive bleeding by improving circulation and strengthening the uterine lining.
remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of dried turmeric powder in two cups of water for five minutes to create a strong infusion.
Reduce the boiling water to simmer and add one tablespoon of honey for added sweetness and antibacterial properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any excess sediment.
Add one teaspoon of freshly squeezed lemon juice to enhance the bioavailability of curcuminoids and improve digestion.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 10-15 minutes before serving, allowing the flavors to meld together harmoniously.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with over bleeding during periods because its extracts possess natural antifibrinolytic properties, which help to regulate blood clotting and platelet function. The tea's flavonoids and polyphenols work synergistically to reduce menstrual flow by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, thereby minimizing inflammation and constriction of uterine vessels. Additionally, maidenhair tree teas' anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm the uterine lining, promoting a more regular and controlled bleeding pattern.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: These properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which may contribute to excessive bleeding.
Astringent and hemostatic effects: The tannins present in Maiden's Hair fern tea can help constrict blood vessels, reduce bleeding, and promote clotting.
Uterine tonic and antispasmodic properties: The plant's estrogenic properties may help regulate uterine contractions and menstrual flow, reducing excessive bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to stop excessive bleeding by constricting blood vessels and promoting clotting.
Barks: Containing tannins that aid in reducing bleeding by contracting blood vessels and promoting platelet formation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves or purchase dried ginkgo biloba leaves in stores.
Combine one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves or two teaspoons of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving.
Drink the cooled tea twice daily, one hour after breakfast and one hour before bedtime, for up to five days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley teas helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural ability to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in parsley work together to calm the uterine lining, reducing heavy menstrual flow and alleviating discomfort. Additionally, parsley's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe cramps and ease the overall symptoms associated with excessive bleeding during periods. By drinking parsley teas regularly, women can experience a significant reduction in bleeding and a more manageable menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Parsley tea contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties of parsley, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, may help alleviate symptoms of heavy menstrual bleeding by reducing uterine inflammation and cramping.

Vitamin K: Parsley is a rich source of vitamin K, which plays a crucial role in blood clotting and platelet function. Drinking parsley tea may help regulate blood clotting and reduce excessive bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in iron and calcium, parsley roots help to stimulate blood clotting and reduce menstrual flow.
Seeds: Packed with omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, parsley seeds help to soothe inflammation and promote uterine health.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried petroselinum crispum leaves into a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a kettle or pot and pour it over the leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the solids.
Drink the tea immediately and consume 2 cups per day, repeating as needed until bleeding subsides.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood teas helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains thujone, a compound that has been traditionally used to reduce menstrual flow and alleviate symptoms of dysmenorrhea. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing the amount of blood lost during menstruation. Additionally, wormwood's antispasmodic effects can ease cramps and discomfort associated with heavy bleeding, promoting a more balanced and manageable period experience. As a result, many women turn to herbal wormwood teas as a natural and effective way to regulate their menstrual cycles.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in wormwood tea help to constrict blood vessels, reducing menstrual flow and alleviating symptoms of heavy bleeding.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in wormwood tea, such as thujone and camphor, have a stimulating effect on the uterine muscles, which can help to reduce excessive bleeding by regulating menstruation.
Fatty acids: Wormwood tea contains fatty acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots have astringent properties, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive menstrual bleeding.
Barks: The bark of the wormwood plant contains tannins, which act as natural antiseptics and help to stop bleeding by reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried artemisia absinthium leaves or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the harvested leaves with 250ml of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then add honey to taste if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times daily, starting from the first day of your period and continuing for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon teas helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural ability to regulate hormones and improve circulation. The tea's active compound, cinnamaldehyde, has been shown to have a positive effect on the uterine lining, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy menstrual cycle. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to excessive bleeding. By drinking this tea regularly, women may experience a reduction in heavy bleeding and a more balanced hormonal response, leading to a more comfortable and manageable period.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the uterine lining, which can contribute to heavy bleeding.
Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content in Ceylon cinnamon tea helps protect the body from free radicals that can damage tissues and exacerbate heavy menstrual bleeding.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Cinnamon's ability to constrict blood vessels may help reduce blood flow to the uterus, which can lead to lighter menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots have been traditionally used to treat heavy menstrual bleeding due to their astringent properties, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow.
Bark: The bark of the Ceylon cinnamon plant contains tannins that can help to reduce excessive bleeding by contracting the uterine lining and reducing blood vessel permeability.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried cinnamomum verum bark, measuring one teaspoon per eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring filtered water to a boil and then reduce heat to a simmer for five minutes.
Steep the cinnamomum verum bark in the hot water for 10-15 minutes or according to package instructions.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Enjoy one to two cups of the warm tea three times a day as needed and store any leftover tea in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with over bleeding during periods because its natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy bleeding. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, also have a vasoconstrictive effect, causing blood vessels to narrow and reducing blood flow to the uterus. This can help to slow down or stop excessive bleeding, providing relief from discomfort and anxiety associated with heavy menstrual periods.
remedy_properties: Vasoconstrictive properties: Ginger's ability to constrict blood vessels helps reduce blood flow to the uterus, which can alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, may help reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and increased bleeding.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage tissues and lead to excessive bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem of the ginger plant, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce heavy menstrual bleeding and alleviate cramps.
Roots: Ginger roots have a natural anticoagulant property that helps regulate blood clotting, reducing excessive bleeding during periods.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots measure out 2-3 teaspoons per cup of water for brewing.
Wash the harvested ginger roots thoroughly under cold running water then scrub with a soft brush.
Chop the cleaned ginger roots into small pieces or slice them thinly to release oils and flavor.
Combine the prepared ginger with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup discard solids and add honey or lemon to taste if desired.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat over bleeding during periods with herbal teas is to brew a strong tea made from herbs such as Yarrow, St. John's Wort, or White Willow bark. Drink 2-3 cups of the tea daily to help constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding. Another option is to combine these herbs with Chamomile to soothe cramps and calm the uterus. This natural approach can provide relief from heavy bleeding and discomfort associated with menstruation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to over bleeding during periods that are treated with herbal teas are digestive issues such as diarrhea, constipation, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal teas like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger can help soothe the digestive system and reduce symptoms. Additionally, herbal teas like dandelion root and turmeric may be used to treat menorrhagia-like bleeding in the stomach or intestines due to conditions such as hemorrhoids or ulcers.
